Overall, Cisco is a good place to work. As a senior systems engineer, I had the opportunity to collaborate with many thoughtful, capable, and genuine people across teamsâfrom engineering and test to compliance, product management, executives, manufacturing, and TAC. The company encourages technical excellence, and there are pockets of genuinely supportive leadership. That said, the experience can vary dramatically...
The company promoted a healthy work-life balance, which was appreciated. Mixed Experience: Positive Culture, Uneven Management

Fantastic resume builder, lovely benefits, remote options, work-life balance, annual sales retreat, recognition programs, continued learning access, growth opportunities, learned a lot from my sales leaders Would work there again
Work life balance; less toxic culture No growth, stagnant company
- Great benefits - Mostly competitive pay - Decent opportunity for movement - Fully remote - Fantastic individual contributor culture Awesome people, ignorant executive leadership

*Generally a very talented administrative community *The community is supported by an Administrative Council *Executives are very supportive of the admin community *Opportunity to further develop your talent Great Administrative Community

Prestigious Global Brand: Working at Cisco offers strong name recognition and credibility on your resume. Smart, Collaborative Teams: Surrounded by talented professionals who are often willing to share knowledge and support cross-functional collaboration. Flexible Work Environment: Hybrid/remote work culture with a focus on results over face time, depending on the team. Great Benefits: Comprehensive healthcare, gene...

My time at Cisco was incredibly rewarding, thanks to the opportunity to collaborate with talented, kind, and highly professional colleagues. The supportive and innovative environment made each project exciting, and the team dynamic was inspiring. I would gladly work with Cisco again, as it was a place where professional growth and meaningful connections thrived. Cisco: A Place of Innovation and Talent
Good community of colleagues and peers Great Colleagues
Supportive work environment and supportive teammates. Very Good
Extra paid days off beyond normal holidays (e.g. 'A day for me' several times a year) Good pay Decent place to work, depending on your politics
Good salary and benefits. You might get a good manager for a short time, but they will inevitably leave or get pushed out. Increasingly toxic workplace in the world of tech

Very little other than a great resume builder to prepare you to go work for companies that promotes empathy that the mighty "buck" is not the end game but just a tool to help make a better world for every one. Gaslighting Culture that promotes solo-ism and status quo
Good culture, people willing to help and advance your career Good if you can be safe from layoffs
Decent pay, good Benefits, free food Good company with fair pay and inclusive culture

in my opinions the Retirement Health insurance benefit is a scam. The overall health benefit is self funded by CISCO. Retires pay 3x the going rate as normal market rates. The company can set the rate an anything it wants as it is self funded. It just shows large subsidy to current employees so it looks appealing to current employees. I would guess that any retire that opts for this plan is actual helping to subsidi...

Constant Leadership changes, Redundant leadership, depending on the manager they can be amazing or unfortunately they can be awful and fall into the stereotypes you see across the tech space. LRs constantly, even after they say "we cut deep enough this time so we don't have to put everyone through this again" just to have another 5 months later. Ups & Downs, Decent Pay, When's the Next LR
Pay Stock appreciation Lack of in-person offices Company performance isn't always reflective of organizational/departmental growth opportunities
